title: storage.ts — sessionId key added to storageKeys
commit_message: feat: define SESSION_ID storage key for device session tracking

why_storage_keys.explanation: Any change to storageKeys affects every test whose symbols[] includes storageKeys: isUserLoggedIn + updateNewTokenDetails (auth-storage), storageKeys alignment (payment-state). Sibling tests in those files are HIGH. utilities.js-only tests SKIP unless the manifest links them to storage for this run policy.
why_storage_keys.key_point: Typical selection: 7 CRITICAL + 10 HIGH = 17; 60 SKIP — similar footprint to scenario 1’s storage slice without renaming TOKEN.

tests.test: isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns false when no token is stored
isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns true after a token has been stored under storageKeys.token
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the access token to localStorage under the TOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the refresh token under the REFRESHTOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the login time under the LOGGEDINTIME key
storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.stripePaymentDetails equals STRIPEPAYMENTDETAILS
storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.token equals TOKEN
tests.result: ⚠️  MAY FAIL — if test hardcodes key list and omits sessionId

HIGH.reason: Same cross-dependent files — remaining describes that share utilities.js or constants.ts with the CRITICAL rows

tests.test: validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes input containing @ to email validation and accepts a valid address
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes plain text input to username validation and accepts a clean username
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the email format is invalid via EMAIL_REGEX
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the username contains special characters
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > output of generateCardNumber preserves first and last 4 digits
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > correctly masks middle digits with X characters
capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > capitalizes the first letter of each word in a multi-word string
capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > capitalizes a single word correctly without whitespace branch
capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > returns undefined when given a non-string truthy value after type guard fix
updateUserCards action dispatched into paymentReducer > populates userCards in state after dispatch
updateUserCards action dispatched into paymentReducer > sets cardsFetched to true when cards are provided

SKIP.reason: storage.ts not in sources[] for standalone regex, utilities-only paths, or feature suites

coverage_gaps.detail: Add an assertion that storageKeys.sessionId === 'SESSION_ID' in auth-storage or payment-state tests.

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "isUserLoggedIn|updateNewTokenDetails|validateEmailOrUsername|generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX|capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace|storageKeys align with payment flow|updateUserCards" --verbose
